title: Conjecture 1.1 (Reed's Conjecture)
statement_text: Every non-null even-hole-free graph has a bisimplicial vertex.
context_text: Reed conjectured that every even-hole-free graph has a bisimplicial vertex (a vertex whose neighbourhood is the union of two cliques). A previous claimed proof by Addario-Berry, Havet, Reed, Chudnovsky, and Seymour [1] was found to contain a serious error in the proof of theorem 3.1 of that paper, making this paper's new proof necessary.
paper_contribution: Provides a complete new proof of the conjecture via two decomposition theorems for even-hole-free graphs, bypassing the flawed step in the earlier proof [1].
paper_title: Even-hole-free graphs still have bisimplicial vertices
